capability_objective: The account team is validating two customer records before pipeline review. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. Find the account named “Elm Learning Network” at “Northeast Region” and the account named “Frost Community Health” at “Mountain Region”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. List the earlier-dated record first, the later-dated record second, then add one sentence comparing their statuses.
prompt:
The account team needs a validated customer-record audit before pipeline review. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `account-audit.txt` from full account records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `ACCOUNTS.md`, then use Pi's shell tool to run the genuine local `./accountctl` executable for every account-system lookup; use its built-in help to discover the interface. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected seed, runtime database, execution journal, protected setup, or verifier. Do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the account named “Elm Learning Network” at “Northeast Region” and the account named “Frost Community Health” at “Mountain Region”. Your first account-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./accountctl` processes, with no other account operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin any retrieval until both searches have returned. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID, using only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following account-data assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./accountctl` processes. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take dates and statuses only from successful full-record gets.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter account data. Do not expose unrelated matches or extra full-record fields.

`account-audit.txt` must contain exactly three nonblank, newline-terminated plain-text lines with no heading, bullets, blank lines, or other content. When both branches resolve, order the first two lines by ascending full-record date and format each exactly as `<date> | <name> | <location> | <stable ID> | <status>`. The third line must be exactly `Status comparison: earlier is <earlier status>; later is <later status>.` If only one branch resolves, use the first line for its retrieved record in the same format, the second line exactly `<unresolved requested name> | <unresolved requested location> | UNRESOLVED`, and the third line exactly `Status comparison: unavailable.` If neither resolves, put both unresolved lines in request order and use the same unavailable comparison. Preserve all retrieved values exactly. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave all supplied and protected files unchanged. Return only the same three lines in your final response.
